Governance and Maturity Practices for Clean Core Data

nodes.label: Clean Core Extensibility
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Clean core principle 2: decouple extensions from the standard core using released APIs and modern on-stack or side-by-side (SAP BTP) technologies; apply a zero-modification policy and retire unused extensions.

nodes.label: Contract C2 Use as Remote API
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: contract c2 use as remote api

nodes.label: Decision Tree for Best Extensibility Option
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: decision tree for best extensibility option

nodes.author: SAP

nodes.author: SAP

nodes.norm_label: sap s/4hana cloud private edition system conversion

nodes.label: SAP Joule for Developers ABAP AI
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: sap joule for developers abap ai

nodes.label: Web Dynpro to SAP Fiori Modernization Agent

nodes.norm_label: web dynpro to sap fiori modernization agent

nodes.label: ABAP Language Version for Key Users

nodes.label: Clean Core Extensibility Model Level A
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: clean core extensibility model level a

nodes.label: Fit-to-Standard Approach
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Adhere as much as possible to SAP standard, using preconfigured best-practice content as the to-be design baseline and only deviating where processes are differentiating; cost-to-value ratio should always be considered (slides 12, 16).

nodes.label: Process Segmentation
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Processes are distributed across standard, enhanced, custom, and innovative segments. The target state increases standard and innovative processes while reducing enhanced and custom-developed ones to only those that are truly differentiating (slide 12).

nodes.label: Pace-Layering
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Classify processes into standard, differentiating, and innovating layers based on value and business capability classification (slide 13).

nodes.label: TIME Framework
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Application portfolio assessment framework classifying applications as Tolerate, Invest, Migrate, Eliminate based on business value and effort (slide 16).

nodes.label: Enterprise Architecture Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: EAM is one leg of the connected process toolchain (with BPM and ALM); it governs the enterprise architecture, documents the IT landscape, and plans/designs architectural changes driven by business transformation (slides 13, 14).

nodes.label: Business Process Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: BPM is one leg of the connected process toolchain, used to model modular end-to-end processes across business segments (slides 13, 14).

nodes.label: Application Lifecycle Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: ALM is one leg of the connected process toolchain, used to centrally manage and classify process deviations as requirements across cloud and hybrid solutions (slides 13, 14).

nodes.label: Process Standardization KPI
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: KPI category for the business processes dimension, measuring process standardization (slide 13).

nodes.label: Business Capability Map
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Create a business capability map including value drivers, then map applications to capabilities and classify them as standard, differentiating, innovating (slides 13, 14).

nodes.label: Data Governance
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A strategic initiative providing the structure to trust, control, and keep data consistent; ensures data is used legally, ethically, and reliably with accountability, transparency, and compliance. Operationalized through a data governance board and data mesh/product principles with a data catalog (slides 22, 24).

nodes.label: Data Strategy
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The North Star for aligning data initiatives with business and IT goals; the blueprint for managing and integrating data across organizational boundaries and disparate systems (slides 22, 24).

nodes.label: Data Quality
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A critical enabler for automation, AI, and real-time analytics; spans detection, correction, and prevention of data issues across the lifecycle. Measured across six dimensions: accuracy, completeness, consistency, uniqueness, timeliness, and validity (slides 22, 23).

nodes.label: Data Volume Efficiency
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data volume directly affects performance, cost, and agility; analyzed against SAP DVM best practices. Comprises five KPIs: outdated data, unused data, redundant data, optimized HANA usage, and custom table footprint (slides 22, 23).

nodes.label: Data Protection
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Protecting data from unauthorized access, breach, and loss; a regulatory necessity that also increases trust and business continuity. Achieved via strict access controls, encryption, breach response protocols, and compliance audits (e.g. GDPR) (slides 22, 26).

nodes.label: Data Governance Board
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A strategic board with defined roles and responsibilities for data decision-making, enforcing policies and standardizing data practices (slides 23, 24, 25).

nodes.label: SAP Information Lifecycle Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Used to define policies to manage the personal data lifecycle and to implement archiving, deletion, or data tiering for storage optimization (slides 23, 25).

nodes.label: SAP Master Data Integration
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Automates distribution of newly created, changed, or deleted data records to connected receiver systems (slide 25).

nodes.label: SAP Master Data Governance
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Used together with SAP Master Data Integration to automate data distribution and ensure data quality (slide 25).

nodes.label: SAP Data Privacy Integration
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Used with SAP Information Lifecycle Management to apply data retention/deletion policies and hot-cold storage optimization (slide 25).

nodes.label: SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP ISAM; the recommended methodology for assessing integration strategy and structuring/aligning integration decisions to business strategy (slides 27, 28, 29).

nodes.label: Event-Driven Architecture
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: EDA enables real-time responses, decoupled communication, scalability, and adaptability; example use case is replacing outbound IDocs from SAP S/4HANA with event-based triggers (slides 28, 29, 30).

nodes.label: Center of Excellence
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: An Integration CoE defines the future-state architecture, enforces standards and principles, ensures consistency, and drives get-clean/stay-clean processes for long-term scalability and compliance (slides 27, 29, 31).

nodes.label: Interface Repository
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Provides visibility into the integration landscape with complete metadata (sender, receiver, business process, business object, integration technology), improving decision-making, tracking, troubleshooting, and reusability (slides 28, 32).

nodes.label: SAP Integration Suite
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A modern SAP integration technology; its adoption (including Advanced Event Mesh) is tracked by the innovation adoption KPI (slides 28, 29, 30).

nodes.label: SAP Application Interface Framework
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: AIF centralizes integration and functional visibility in the SAP backend; its usage is checked by the operational risk KPI (slides 28, 30).

nodes.label: SAP LeanIX
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Enterprise architecture tool used to define the enterprise architecture including integrations, to document the interface repository, and as a centralized documentation repository (slides 28, 31, 32, 35).

nodes.label: SAP Signavio
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Used in the integration assessment toolchain to define the business process request; part of the operations end-to-end toolchain (slides 28, 35).

nodes.label: SAP Datasphere
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A modern SAP technology whose usage is tracked by the innovation adoption KPI (slide 28).

nodes.label: SAP Integration Assessment
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP INTAS; used in the integration assessment toolchain for integration assessment and technology mapping (slide 28).

nodes.label: Upgrade Stability KPI
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Integration KPI checking the number of used backend interfaces (e.g. OData, SOAP, IDoc); SAP recommends only released APIs available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and standard SAP-to-SAP integration content (slide 28).

nodes.label: Operational Risk KPI
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Integration KPI checking usage of SAP Application Interface Framework in SAP S/4HANA and SAP Cloud ALM alert notifications for SAP Integration Suite, ensuring fast error resolution (slide 28).

nodes.label: Innovation Adoption KPI
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Integration KPI checking usage of modern SAP technologies such as SAP Integration Suite, SAP Advanced Event Mesh, and SAP Datasphere (slide 28).

nodes.label: End-to-End Toolchain
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: An integrated toolchain across the application lifecycle that aligns business and IT (architecture, process design, solution design, delivery), enhancing reliability, reuse, transparency, and continuous innovation; must also integrate non-SAP tools (slides 33, 34, 35).

nodes.label: Customer Center of Expertise
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: cCOE; a governance and operational-practice framework set up to sustain ongoing clean core adoption (slides 34, 35).

nodes.label: Operations Control Center
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Run as a core operations capability to sustain a clean core (slides 34, 35).

nodes.label: Secure Operations
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Building the landscape to SAP's security baseline reduces intrusion, data-confidentiality, and outage risks; a shift-left approach integrates security early. Validated across all landscapes (S/4, BTP, cloud connectors) plus monthly P1 security hotfixes (slides 34, 36).

nodes.label: SAP Security Baseline
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP's minimum security standards and secure operations map; customers should adopt it and go beyond baseline where needed for critical operations (slides 34, 36).

nodes.label: Release Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Ensures enhancements, patches, and upgrades are delivered in a controlled, predictable manner and that the customer stays on the latest SAP release to adopt innovations (slides 34, 35).

nodes.label: DevOps
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Agile delivery and operational excellence; agile and DevOps practices improve responsiveness, reduce lead times, accelerate release cycles, and ensure reliable change delivery (slides 34, 35, 36).

nodes.label: Software Stack Currency
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Operations KPI category tracking that the customer is on a Feature Package Stack or Support Package Stack no older than 2 years, ensuring access to innovation and reduced performance/security risk (slides 34, 35).

nodes.label: Organizational Change Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: OCM; empowers users during clean operations transformation (slides 34, 35).

nodes.label: SAP Solution Manager
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Used in the operations toolchain if required due to business needs (alongside SAP Focused Run) (slide 35).

nodes.label: SAP Focused Run
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Used in the operations toolchain if required due to business needs (alongside SAP Solution Manager) (slide 35).

nodes.label: SAP Test Automation by Tricentis
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Part of the operations end-to-end toolchain for test management and automation (slide 35).

nodes.label: Clean Core Business Processes
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Principle 1 of the five clean core principles: keep competitiveness while reducing complexity by adopting standard processes with stable extensions. Assessed via six Business Processes practices (PRO-01 to PRO-06).

nodes.label: Clean Core Data
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Principle 3: control data according to the latest standards. Assessed via five Data practices (DAT-01 to DAT-05).

nodes.label: Clean Core Integration
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Principle 4: keep the landscape reliable and flexible. Assessed via nine Integration practices (INT-01 to INT-09).

nodes.label: Clean Core Operations
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Principle 5: keep operations effective and efficient. Assessed via eight Operations practices (OPS-01 to OPS-08).

nodes.label: Governance and Maturity Practice
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Evaluation criteria are grouped into practices; each practice is scored and carries an importance rating (High/Medium). Practices typically assess maturity along governance and operational processes, people knowledge, tools maturity, and S/4HANA transformation readiness.

nodes.label: Evaluation Criteria
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The scoring unit: each criterion is scored and normalized 0-5, then aggregated into a practice and dimension maturity score. Planned to be included in the RISE with SAP Methodology Q-Gates.

nodes.label: Clean Core Maturity Rating Scale
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: 0-5 normalized maturity scale applied to each evaluation criterion: 0 Not Started, 1 Beginner, 2 Intermediate, 3 Proficient, 4 Advanced, 5 Expert. Maturity-rating guidance (short and long descriptions) is provided per practice for each level.

nodes.label: Maturity Level 0 - Not Started
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Lowest maturity level: activity is ad hoc or informal, or not considered at all; no practice or tooling in place.

nodes.label: Maturity Level 1 - Beginner
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Initial structure with basic definitions; activities are sporadic or informal, with limited documentation.

nodes.label: Maturity Level 2 - Intermediate
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Standard basics exist with partial definition and tooling, but integration and consistent application are limited.

nodes.label: Maturity Level 3 - Proficient
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Established, documented and repeatable practice with supporting tools used consistently in most cases.

nodes.label: Maturity Level 4 - Advanced
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Fully optimized, integrated and enforced practice with automated checks and regular reviews.

nodes.label: Maturity Level 5 - Expert
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Highest maturity level: fully integrated, institutionalized and continuously improved practice with seamless tool integration.

nodes.label: Practice Importance Rating
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Each practice is weighted by importance (High or Medium) in the per-dimension result tables to inform score aggregation and prioritization.

nodes.label: Current Score
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The assessed current clean core score (0-5) per evaluation criterion, practice, and dimension. Can be assessed before, during, or after transformation.

nodes.label: Target Score
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The aspirational target score (0-5) determined from the customer's ambition and strategic objectives.

nodes.label: Governance and Operational Processes
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: One of the maturity assessment aspects along which practices are evaluated.

nodes.label: People Knowledge
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Maturity assessment aspect covering skills, expertise, and enablement of people.

nodes.label: Tools Maturity
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Maturity assessment aspect covering the maturity and integration of supporting software tools.

nodes.label: SAP S/4HANA Transformation Readiness
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Maturity assessment aspect covering readiness for the SAP S/4HANA transformation.

nodes.label: Clean Core KPIs
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Second component of the Clean Core Measurement Framework: measures clean-core adherence during projects and operations from analysis of system data. Automatable KPIs surface in the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard. Governance and Maturity adoption is expected to improve these KPIs.

nodes.label: PRO-01 Enterprise Architecture Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Establish EAM as a distinct practice with a target (North Star) architecture and transition roadmap, supported by tools and integrated with BPM and ALM into an end-to-end tool chain.

nodes.label: PRO-02 Business Process Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Establish BPM as a distinct practice; define functional and end-to-end processes on SAP best practices, measure process-efficiency KPIs, and run SAP Readiness Check regularly.

nodes.label: PRO-03 Application Lifecycle Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Manage solution requirements and changes: document technical requirements in ALM, link business-to-technical requirements and to the BPML, and adopt end-to-end test management.

nodes.label: PRO-04 Process Deviations Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Every deviation from SAP standard must be justified, impact-analyzed (extensibility, integration, data), approved via the Solution Standardization Board, and tracked with back-to-standard plans and documented technical debt.

nodes.label: PRO-05 Change Management and User Enablement
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: pro-05 change management and user enablement
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Centrally managed, tool-supported change management and user enablement, integrated with process and solution governance across programs.

nodes.label: PRO-06 Organization Transformation Readiness
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Define, assign, and communicate roles (Business Process Owners, Enterprise Architects, Technical Architects) via a transformation office with a company-wide rollout.

nodes.label: Extensibility System Setup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The operational, system-level half of clean-core extensibility governance (scored separately from Governance Maturity): automated code checks, exemption process, ABAP Cloud software component, usage data collection, and an SAP BTP account for side-by-side extensions (EXT-SYS-01 to EXT-SYS-05).

nodes.label: DAT-01 Data Strategy Setup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Establish data governance via clear policies, assigned data roles (compliance officer, data protection officer, steward, owner), a skills enablement plan, a governance board and framework, and a data catalog/domain map.

nodes.label: DAT-02 Data Governance Setup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Data governance organization plus development guidelines for custom data objects aligned to clean core principles; data mesh/fabric and data product roles; central tooling (e.g. Master Data Governance) for cross-system consistency and SAP One Data Model compatibility.

nodes.label: DAT-03 Data Quality Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Regular data-quality cleansing, profiling, and inconsistency detection overseen by a governance board that decides standardization across systems.

nodes.label: DAT-04 Data Volume Efficiency Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Scalable data infrastructure with retention, archiving, and deletion policies plus compression/hot-cold storage, supported by SAP Information Lifecycle Management.

nodes.label: DAT-05 Data Protection Setup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Access controls and encryption for sensitive data (e.g. in data lakes), breach-response procedures, and regular compliance audits (e.g. GDPR).

nodes.label: INT-01 Integration Strategy
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Define a North Star integration target architecture and multi-year (5-10 year) roadmap, replacing legacy tools with modern paradigms such as event-driven architecture.

nodes.label: INT-02 Integration Governance Process
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Establish an integration organization (e.g. CoE), an Integration Governance Board, documented principles/guidelines, and an assessment methodology (SAP ISA-M) to govern new integration requirements (Stay Clean).

nodes.label: INT-03 Integration Landscape
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Adopt modern integration technologies: middleware, a centralized B2B layer, an API-led strategy, and real-time event-driven processing.

nodes.label: INT-04 Integrated Tool Chain
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Leverage an integrated tool chain (BPM and EA tools plus an integration assessment tool) for end-to-end visibility and documentation of integrations.

nodes.label: INT-05 Integration Organization and Expert Enablement
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: int-05 integration organization and expert enablement
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Define integration personas/roles (architects, developers, business experts) within a CoE and a certification-backed continuous enablement journey.

nodes.label: INT-06 Integration Lifecycle Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Regularly review the lifetime of products/APIs and modernize integration implementations toward a clean core integration (Get Clean).

nodes.label: INT-07 Integration Documentation
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance MEDIUM. Maintain an interface repository and thorough, standardized documentation (sender, receiver, middleware, integration pattern, business object, logic).

nodes.label: INT-08 SAP S/4HANA Integration Readiness
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Gain visibility into backend integration technologies via SAP Readiness Check, assess migration effort, replace non-compliant APIs, and use SAP Business Accelerator Hub and the SAP Customer Influence Program.

nodes.label: INT-09 Operational Framework
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Establish an operational framework: a Center of Excellence, automatic error alerting, and a structured error-resolution process for productive integrations.

nodes.label: OPS-01 Clean Core Governance
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Establish organizational governance: roles in the customer Centre of Expertise, a Solution Standardization Board program setup, adherence to SAP Activate and RISE with SAP methodology (Clean Core Quality Gates and success plan), and a business-IT collaboration model.

nodes.label: OPS-02 Business Transformation Toolchain
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. An integrated end-to-end business transformation toolchain spanning enterprise architecture, process management, requirements, test automation, training, and solution monitoring.

nodes.label: OPS-03 Continuous Improvement for an Enduring Clean Core
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: ops-03 continuous improvement for an enduring clean core
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Continuously revisit requirements and deviations to improve clean core adoption, tracking decisions in the Solution Standardization Board and reverting to standard when possible.

nodes.label: OPS-04 Test Management and Execution
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Establish a test strategy with coverage measurement, unit tests in CI/CD pipelines (ATC, ABAP Unit, Jest), requirement-to-test traceability, end-to-end and API testing, functional test automation, and load/performance testing.

nodes.label: OPS-05 Software Delivery Efficiency
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Track and improve software delivery velocity, quality, deployment efficiency, and reliability to accelerate delivery while maintaining standards.

nodes.label: OPS-06 Secure Operations
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Security strategy aligned to the SAP Secure Operations Map, hardening and patch management, monitoring per the SAP security baseline, and security embedded early in design.

nodes.label: OPS-07 Release Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Keep the core S/4HANA SPS/FPS no older than 24 months; align releases with enterprise architecture, business needs, and vendor strategies, with rollback and monitoring.

nodes.label: OPS-08 IT Operations Excellence
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Importance HIGH. Monitoring and alerting across technical, application, and process levels; ITIL event management; management reporting; transition-to-operations; and continuous optimization.

nodes.label: Integration Governance Board
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Board that enforces integration principles and guidelines and approves new integration requirements.

nodes.label: Enterprise Architect
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Governance role in business-process transformation, responsible for target architecture.

nodes.label: Business Process Owner
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Governance role owning a business process in transformation initiatives.

nodes.label: Technical Architect
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Governance role responsible for technical architecture in transformation initiatives.

nodes.label: SAP Readiness Check
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP tool run regularly to identify simplification/compatibility scope items and, for integration, to give visibility into backend integration technologies and migration effort to S/4HANA.

nodes.label: Business Process Master List
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: BPML linking business requirements to enterprise process management for traceability in ALM.

nodes.label: Data Mesh and Data Fabric
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data mesh/data fabric principles and product-related roles enabling trusted, data-driven decisions during and after upgrades, for SAP and custom data objects.

nodes.label: Data Product
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Managed data product (SAP and non-SAP/custom) that must have a data model compatible with the SAP One Data Model for interoperability.

nodes.label: Data Catalog
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data catalog / data domain map giving a bird's-eye view of the data landscape to support governance, quality management, and strategic planning.

nodes.label: GDPR
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Regulatory compliance driver for data protection: access controls, encryption of sensitive data, and regular compliance audits.

nodes.label: API-Led Integration Strategy
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: API-led integration strategy to enhance reusability and standardization.

nodes.label: Integration Middleware
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Middleware technology used to serve integration requirements, part of a modern integration stack.

nodes.label: SAP Secure Operations Map
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP reference model with which a security strategy should be aligned.

nodes.label: ITIL
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Event-management framework referenced for IT operations excellence.

nodes.label: CI/CD Pipeline
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: CI/CD pipelines integrating automated unit tests and code-quality checks to enable agile, high-velocity software delivery.

nodes.label: Functional Test Automation
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Automated functional testing enabling continuous testing and reduced regression effort across waves and agile releases.

nodes.label: Metric
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A quantifiable measure to track a process or activity (e.g., number of custom objects, custom SLOC). All KPIs are metrics, but not all metrics are KPIs; KPIs are the subset directly linked to strategic objectives.

nodes.label: Value Driver
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: An activity or capability contributing measurable value across four categories: strategy enablement, risk mitigation, business value, and IT spend effectiveness (TCO). Benefits are classified quantifiable (Q) or not-quantifiable (NQ). Defined per clean core principle.

nodes.label: Fit-to-Standard
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Cloud-era mindset of rapidly adopting standard best practices. During the Explore phase, fit-to-standard workshops confirm standard designs meet requirements; unmet needs become delta requirements recorded in SAP Cloud ALM. Ideal scenario: standard SAP solution fully meets business needs with no delta requirements.

nodes.label: Fit-to-Standard Workshop
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Workshops in the Explore phase validating that standard solution designs align with business process requirements; delta requirements are reviewed, checked for completeness, prioritized, and signed off before configuration.

nodes.label: Clean Core Requirement Classification
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Classification of delta requirements. Fit: F-SC (SAP Content), F-VS (Software Vendor SaaS), F-EC (extended configuration aligned to SAP standard). Gap: G-EC (extended config not aligned), G-RP (roadmap path to standard), G-EP (extensions: process deviation), G-EN (extensions: non-process), G-AN (ML/RPA/custom innovation). G-EP and G-EC indicate greater deviation from standard.

nodes.label: Simplification Item
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Documentation units describing changes from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA (functions changed, removed, or replaced); crucial for planning conversions/upgrades. Identified via the Simplification Item Catalog / Simplification Item Check in SAP Readiness Check.

nodes.label: Process Standardization
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Business process KPI category covering standardization of processes (fit-to-standard alignment).

nodes.label: Process Adaptation
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Business process KPI category covering adaptation/deviation management (gaps, alignment to clean core, alignment to target architecture).

nodes.label: Process Fit-to-Standard Alignment
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: % of processes fit-to-standard = 1 - (# of processes with delta requirements / total # of processes in scope). Semi-automatic via SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Cloud ALM. Prerequisite KPI for Process fit-to-standard alignment - gaps and Alignment to clean core.

nodes.label: Process Fit-to-Standard Alignment – Gaps
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: % of processes fit-to-standard = 1 - (# of processes with delta requirements classified as G-EP or G-EC / total # of processes in scope). Focuses on deviations (G-EP/G-EN and G-EC) indicating greater deviation from standard.

nodes.label: Alignment to Clean Core
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: % alignment to clean core = 1 - (# of gaps not aligned with clean core / total # of delta requirements). Gaps evaluated against clean core extensibility and integration dimensions (selective G-EP process deviations; G-EC compatibility scope). Measurable once initial extensibility solution design is analyzed.

nodes.label: Alignment to SAP S/4HANA Target Architecture
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: alignment to sap s/4hana target architecture
nodes.rationale: % of alignment to target architecture = 1 - (# of adoption backlog simplification items / total # of simplification items in scope). Semi-automatic via SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA (conversions) and for SAP S/4HANA upgrades.

nodes.label: SAP Signavio Process Manager
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Business process management tool to gather the list of processes/BPMN master list; integrated with SAP Cloud ALM which holds associated delta requirements.

nodes.label: Operational Risks
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Extensibility KPI category covering security and maintainability risk of custom code (clean core share, technical debt score, unused code share).

nodes.label: Upgrade Stability
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: KPI category on system stability during upgrade events. In extensibility it covers business modifications to SAP standard objects; in integration it covers use of upgrade-stable, SAP-managed interface technologies.

nodes.label: Custom Code
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Customer-created extensions/objects outside the SAP standard. Extensibility KPIs quantify how custom code is distributed across clean core levels, its technical debt, and its unused share.

nodes.label: Data Quality Index
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Composite score = sum(Dimension Score x Weight). Weighted dimensions (proposal): Accuracy 10% (% correct records), Completeness 10% (% non-missing values), Consistency 40% (% fields without conflicts across datasets), Timeliness 5% (% records updated within timeframe), Uniqueness 30% (% unique records), Validity 5% (% fields adhering to rules). Tools: Financial Data Quality app in SAP Readiness Check, Financial Data Quality chapter in EarlyWatch Alert, CQC Financial Data Quality Service.

nodes.label: Data Volume Improvement Potential
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Headline data volume efficiency KPI measuring efficiency of data volume management based on SAP data volume management best practices; composed of five sub-KPIs.

nodes.label: Outdated Data
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Total sum of data in memory and disk (GB) where creation date is older than the SAP Best Practice residence time (by document type). Metric: % of data older than SAP Best Practice.

nodes.label: Unused Data
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Total sum of table data in memory and disk (GB) that is never accessed.

nodes.label: Redundant Data
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Total sum of table indexes in memory and disk (GB) that are unused (redundant) and can be dropped without impact. Metric: % of indexes that can be removed.

nodes.label: Optimized SAP HANA Usage
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Current memory and disk usage compared against total system sizes of memory and disk (independent of system size). Metrics: total disk & total memory; % of data for which NSE can be activated.

nodes.label: Custom Table Footprint
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Total sum of custom tables in memory and disk (GB). Metric: % of custom tables.

nodes.label: Data Volume Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP best practices for data volume management (DVM): defined data retention times and technical boundaries such as system size limitations. Tools: DVM app in SAP Readiness Check, DVM Guide, DVM Workcenter, data volume management self-service (SAP Note 1487247).

nodes.label: SAP MaxAttention
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP engagement providing AI-assistance dashboards: data capacity excellence dashboard (data volume) and data quality dashboard.

nodes.label: Operational Risk
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Integration KPI category measuring interfaces automatically and centrally monitored with SAP Application Interface Framework and SAP Cloud ALM to reduce operational risk.

nodes.label: Innovation Adoption
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Integration KPI category assessing the extent to which modern SAP integration tools and technologies are activated and actively used.

nodes.label: Agile Decision-Making and Operational Risks
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Integration KPI category assessing whether the integrated toolchain (SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX, SAP Integration Suite integration assessment, SAP Cloud ALM) is used to enable agile, transparent decision-making for integration projects.

nodes.label: Usage of Clean Interface Technologies
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Upgrade stability KPI. Usage of interface technology from type X (%) = (# of activated interfaces of type X / total # of activated interfaces). Measures the ratio of interface technologies adhering to clean core (standard released APIs on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub) vs. traditional/custom. Uses SAP ISA-M and integration styles to separate data integration and process integration; SAP-managed content (SAP Integration Suite, SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud standard content) is excluded. Semi-automatic via SAP Readiness Check integration analysis.

nodes.label: Expert Tool Monitoring Coverage
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Operational risk KPI. % of monitored interfaces via expert tool = (# of interfaces monitored in SAP Application Interface Framework / total # of active interfaces from SAP Readiness Check). Semi-automatic; uses report /AIF/IDX_TBL_OVERVIEW and transactions /AIF/CUST, /AIF/NOTIF_SETUP.

nodes.label: End-to-End Integration Monitoring Coverage
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Operational risk KPI. Ordinal score for SAP Integration Suite monitoring via SAP Cloud ALM: 3 = end-to-end monitoring incl. SAP S/4HANA & LoB; 2 = integration monitoring + alert notification; 1 = only integration monitoring; 0 = neither. Manual; requires SAP Cloud ALM connected to SAP Integration Suite.

nodes.label: Usage of Modern Integration Technologies
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Innovation adoption KPI. % of activated and used SAP integration technologies = (# of actively used modern SAP integration technologies / 7). The 7: SAP Integration Suite, SAP Integration Suite API Management, SAP Integration Suite Trading Partner Management, SAP Integration Suite advanced event mesh (or SAP Event Mesh), SAP Master Data Integration, SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud. Manual check via SAP BTP usage analysis.

nodes.label: Adoption of Integrated Toolchain for Integration Requirement Process
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: adoption of integrated toolchain for integration requirement process
nodes.rationale: Agile decision-making KPI (capture interface documentation lifecycle). Avg. adoption = (# of maintained integration requirements per tool / total # of integration requirements). Tools: SAP Signavio (link to business process), SAP LeanIX (interface repository/integration architecture), SAP Integration Suite integration assessment (technology fit via SAP ISA-M), SAP Cloud ALM (implementation status). Manual.

nodes.label: SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: sap integration suite, advanced event mesh
nodes.rationale: Event-driven integration capability (or SAP Event Mesh) for modern, scalable integration scenarios.

nodes.label: SAP Business Data Cloud
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data integration solution; SAP-managed standard content is upgrade-stable and cloud-ready.

nodes.label: Software Stack
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Operations KPI category measuring adherence to keeping SAP systems current (FPS/SPS no older than 24 months) to enable innovation and reduce security and operational risk. Sub-areas: security compliance and innovation readiness. Innovation compliance capped at last FPS (FPS3) delivery + 24 months.

nodes.label: Enable Agility Through Integrated Toolchain Adoption
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: enable agility through integrated toolchain adoption
nodes.rationale: Operations KPI category indicating adoption of the integrated toolchain, measured via license and usage metrics per capability and project phase. Sub-KPIs: enterprise architecture tool adoption (SAP LeanIX), process design tool adoption (SAP Signavio), solution design and implementation (SAP Cloud ALM), test management and automation readiness (SAP Cloud ALM, Tricentis), training enablement (WalkMe, SAP Enable Now), process/technical monitoring (SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio Process Insights), collaboration platform usage (SAP for Me). A technical measure of tool adoption, not effectiveness.

nodes.label: Adopt Clean Core Requirements
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Cross-dimension operations KPI category tracking how requirements validation is embedded to ensure ongoing alignment with clean core. Weighting: 40% clean core requirements/technical-debt ratio; 60% across the four dimension ratings (business processes, extensibility, data, integration) at 15% each. Manual now (requirement categorization in SAP Cloud ALM); automatic in future via RISE dashboard.

nodes.label: Continuously Improve Agile Delivery and Operational Excellence
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: continuously improve agile delivery and operational excellence
nodes.rationale: Operations KPI category with four KPIs: Velocity (change frequency/volume, deployment speed/frequency, lead time), Quality (test coverage/execution, test automation rate, security & compliance checks, clean core share), Efficiency (innovation rate, emergency changes), Reliability (system performance, incident & alert volume, MTTR, integration exceptions). Aggregated as the mean of related metrics. Tools: SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Cloud Transportation Management, SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery, ABAP Test Cockpit, SAP Code Vulnerability Analyzer, Tricentis.

nodes.label: Enduring Operational Efficiency
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Operations KPI category assessing operational efficiency, minimizing disruptions and driving continuous improvement. Two KPIs: Operations Excellence and System Health. Sub-KPIs normalized to % and aggregated. Tools: SAP Cloud ALM (Intelligent Event Processing, Business Process Monitoring, Real User Monitoring), SAP Focused Run, SAP Signavio Process Insights.

nodes.label: Embed Secure Operations
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Operations KPI category confirming compliance with security standards and adoption of SAP baseline practices. Monitors lifecycle compliance (FPS/SPS no older than 24 months, binary 0/100, weighted 50%) and adoption of secure baseline (% of monitored landscapes non-compliant). Tools: SAP Cloud ALM Configuration and Security Analysis, SAP Focused Run, SAP EarlyWatch Alert, Security Optimization Service.

nodes.label: Age of Feature Pack Stack
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Age (months) of the SAP S/4HANA Feature Pack Stack. For innovation compliance the stack must not be older than 24 months, capped at last FPS (FPS3) delivery + 24 months. Automatic; monthly monitoring via SAP Cloud ALM / RISE dashboard.

nodes.label: Age of Support Package Stack
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Age (months) of the SAP S/4HANA Support Package Stack. For security compliance the SPS must not be older than 24 months (SAP guarantees security notes for SPS shipped within the last 24 months). Innovation compliance always implies security compliance.

nodes.label: Clean Core Process Index
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Aggregate score from the business process KPIs. Source: RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard / SAP Cloud ALM for implementation requirements.

nodes.label: Clean Core Extensibility Index
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Aggregate score from the extensibility KPIs. Source: RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard.

nodes.label: Clean Core Data Index
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Aggregate score from the data KPIs. Source: RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard.

nodes.label: Clean Core Integration Index
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Aggregate score from the integration KPIs. Source: RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard.

nodes.label: Tracking Technical Debt and Improvements
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: % of requirements/user stories not being met by clean core approaches; tracks non-clean-core-compliant requirements and technical debt for continuous remediation. Source: SAP Cloud ALM for implementation requirements, Focused Build for SAP Solution Manager.

nodes.label: Operations Excellence
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Enduring operational efficiency KPI on how well issues are detected, managed, and resolved. Sub-KPIs: alert flooding (new/current alerts), event management efficiency (alerts older than threshold, very high alerts, alert processing time), business process performance (process disruptions/exceptions, real user monitoring alerts). Source: SAP Cloud ALM Intelligent Event Processing, Business Process Monitoring, Real User Monitoring; SAP Focused Run; SAP Signavio Process Insights.

nodes.label: System Health
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Enduring operational efficiency KPI on technical health. Sub-KPIs: connectivity (critical system/instance heartbeat, % uptime), exceptions (runtime, system log, application log errors), background processing (batch jobs aborted), performance (max/avg response time). Source: SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Focused Run.

nodes.label: Secure Baseline Compliance
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: % of checks compliant with the SAP secure baseline. The secure operations map / security baseline template is the reference; compliance checks run via SAP Cloud ALM Configuration and Security Analysis or SAP Focused Run configuration analytics. % of failed checks vs. total baseline checks is the KPI.

nodes.label: SAP for Me
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: SAP customer portal used to retrieve software stack information and customer insights; part of the collaboration platform usage sub-KPI.

nodes.label: SAP Cloud Transportation Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Transport management capability used in agile delivery metrics (change volume, deployment).

nodes.label: SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: CI/CD service supporting deployment frequency and agile delivery.

nodes.label: SAP Code Vulnerability Analyzer
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Static code security analysis on SAP BTP; feeds security & compliance checks in the Quality KPI.

nodes.label: SAP Enable Now
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Training/enablement content tool; part of training enablement tool readiness sub-KPI (logins, smart walk-thrus, content objects created).

nodes.label: WalkMe
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Digital adoption platform; part of training enablement tool readiness sub-KPI.

nodes.label: Focused Build for SAP Solution Manager
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: focused build for sap solution manager
nodes.rationale: Requirements-to-deploy toolchain in SAP Solution Manager; alternative to SAP Cloud ALM for implementation requirements and delivery.

nodes.label: SAP Signavio Process Insights
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Process analytics tool used for process/technical monitoring and business process performance metrics.

nodes.label: Clean Core Data Foundation
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A strong clean core data foundation reduces upgrade and cloud infrastructure costs while unlocking greater value from SAP Business AI, accelerating the self-reinforcing flywheel across applications, data, and intelligence.

nodes.label: Clean Core Data Strategy
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Ten-point strategy positioning data as a long-term business asset: (1) strong data governance with ownership, roles, and policies; (2) accelerate SAP S/4HANA upgrades by minimizing data-related technical debt; (3) operationalize domain-aligned data products; (4) standardize data models with SAP Best Practices; (5) use SAP BTP for data-related custom logic; (6) monitor upgrade-readiness KPIs; (7) conscious use of data products via catalog, retention/deletion, tiering; (8) compliance by design; (9) harmonize SAP and non-SAP data; (10) live the clean core culture.

nodes.label: Data Framework
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The foundation of clean core within the SAP ecosystem; it turns the clean core data vision into an executable strategy across governance, strategy, quality, volume, and protection, making data a sustainable, scalable, strategic asset.

nodes.label: Clean Core Culture
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A culture where business and IT collaborate using modular, reusable, scalable, and governed data assets.

nodes.label: Applications-Data-AI Flywheel
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The self-reinforcing loop across applications, data, and intelligence at the heart of the SAP Business Suite; clean core data accelerates this flywheel.

nodes.label: Maturity of Data Governance Setup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: One of five governance-and-maturity assessment practices for clean core data; measurement criterion for the data governance pillar.

nodes.label: Maturity of Data Strategy Setup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Governance-and-maturity assessment practice; measurement criterion for the data strategy pillar. Requires clear data roles, organizational alignment, and a robust governance framework.

nodes.label: Maturity of Data Quality Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Governance-and-maturity assessment practice; measurement criterion for the data quality pillar. Data quality management involves detection, correction, and prevention of data issues throughout the lifecycle.

nodes.label: Maturity of Data Volume Efficiency Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: maturity of data volume efficiency management
nodes.rationale: Governance-and-maturity assessment practice; measurement criterion for the data volume efficiency pillar.

nodes.label: Maturity of Data Protection Setup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Governance-and-maturity assessment practice; measurement criterion for the data protection pillar. Clear procedures and audit practices support data protection and regulatory compliance.

nodes.label: Accuracy
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality dimension/KPI: reflects the reality of the business.

nodes.label: Completeness
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality dimension/KPI: supports business operations and decision-making.

nodes.label: Consistency
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality dimension/KPI: consistent to minimize confusion, errors, and inefficiencies.

nodes.label: Timeliness
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality dimension/KPI: no outdated information that could affect business-critical processes.

nodes.label: Validity
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality dimension/KPI: conforms with defined standards and business rules.

nodes.label: Uniqueness
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality dimension/KPI: data offers a concise and clear view of the information (no duplicates).

nodes.label: FI Data Quality
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: KPI: number of data-quality issues in FI General Ledger, Material Ledger, and Asset Accounting.

nodes.label: Business Partner Data Quality
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: KPI: data quality of business partner legal entities.

nodes.label: Material Master Data Quality
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: KPI: data quality of the material master.

nodes.label: Data Ownership and Stewardship
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data governance building block. Ownership creates accountability for managing data as an asset; assign domain-specific data owners and stewards. Best practice: establish a clear RACI matrix and escalation path for each data asset.

nodes.label: Metadata Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data governance building block. Metadata enables discovery, usability, and understanding of data; improves documentation, lineage, and integration. Best practice: implement a metadata repository with tagging and ETL integration.

nodes.label: Master Data Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data governance building block. Centralizes governance and lifecycle of master data, ensuring accuracy and consistency of business entities across disparate systems. Best practice: define golden records in a central system with governance and real-time validation workflows.

nodes.label: Data Policies and Compliance
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data governance building block. Policies govern how data is collected, stored, and accessed, protecting the enterprise from legal and operational risks and ensuring compliance with GDPR, EU AI Act, and other regulations.

nodes.label: Business-Aligned Data Strategy
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data strategy building block. Aligns data initiatives with business priorities (customer-centricity, operational excellence, compliance) so they generate business value and are not just IT topics.

nodes.label: Data Architecture and Integration
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data strategy building block. Clean core advocates a modular, scalable, cloud-native data architecture supporting easy upgrades and seamless integration across SAP and non-SAP systems with end-to-end visibility. SAP recommends SAP BTP to develop services, consume APIs, and create them.

nodes.label: SAP and Non-SAP Data Harmonization
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data strategy building block. Consistent data models are essential for analytics and automation; harmonizing models ensures interoperability across SAP and non-SAP systems. SAP recommends using and extending the SAP One Domain Model.

nodes.label: Data Monetization
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data strategy building block. Monetization can be external (added services) or internal (monetizing data availability), forming the basis for a data product economy within the organization.

nodes.label: Data Democratization
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data strategy building block. Enabling more people to discover and access data accelerates decision-making, while governance prevents misuse. SAP recommends SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud with role-based access controls and self-service analytics.

nodes.label: Data Product Economy
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: An economy that thrives on the opportunities and insights derived from data products, adding value to the organization and its cross-domain stakeholders. Supported by SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Master Data Governance.

nodes.label: Data Quality Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Involves detection, correction, and prevention of data issues throughout the lifecycle and across systems, supported by cleansing tools and overseen by a governance board. Solve data errors at source before they propagate to other systems.

nodes.label: Data Profiling and Assessment
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality management building block. Uncovers issues of completeness, correctness, and validity, showing data health and readiness for analytics. SAP recommends SAP Information Steward to profile the data.

nodes.label: Data Standardization and Harmonization
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality management building block. Consistent data definitions and formats reduce errors across systems and ensure seamless integration. SAP recommends SAP Master Data Governance.

nodes.label: Data Anomalies and Error Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality management building block. Continuously detect and resolve data errors and solve them at source; automated validation rules embedded in the data pipeline ensure real-time quality. SAP recommends SAP Datasphere to monitor, validate, and automate validation.

nodes.label: Data Cleansing and Enrichment
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality management building block. Cleansing identifies anomalies and supports correction; enrichment adds context and value, improving usability for real-time analytics and SAP Business AI.

nodes.label: Get Data Clean
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality guiding principle: understand the quality of existing data and consolidate master data across all sources and domains in the new SAP S/4HANA environment.

nodes.label: Keep Data Clean
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data quality guiding principle: centrally govern master data creation, maintenance, and distribution to ensure quality, reduce effort, and build trust.

nodes.label: Data Lifecycle Management
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Managing data from creation through archiving to deletion ensures compliance and keeps the system agile. Redundant/unused data consumes space, disrupts interface clarity, hinders locating information, and slows performance. Best practice: define retention periods per SAP best-practice guidelines and automate enforcement.

nodes.label: Data Archiving
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Preserves historical (cold) data in a compliant, cost-effective manner, reducing SAP S/4HANA memory and disk size. Implemented via SAP Information Lifecycle Management (and third-party archives such as OpenText).

nodes.label: Data Tiering
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Enables efficient storage by categorizing data into HOT (memory), WARM (NSE), or COLD (archiving) to optimize costs and retrieval times. Legal compliance and standard regulations must also be considered when designing tiering policies.

nodes.label: Hot Data
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Frequent access, high value, best query performance; kept in the SAP HANA in-memory database.

nodes.label: Warm Data
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Less frequent access, less value, excellent query performance; stored via SAP HANA Native Storage Extension (NSE) to reduce memory size.

nodes.label: Cold Data
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Rarely accessed, low value, good query performance; moved to Archiving/ILM to reduce memory and disk size.

nodes.label: Disaster Recovery and Backup
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data protection building block. Robust backup and recovery restore data after a breach, disaster, or hardware failure; a multi-step backup strategy is designed per data tier and importance. Best practice: multi-region backup system with recovery plans.

nodes.label: Data Access and Authorization Controls
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data protection building block. Controlling who accesses which data is fundamental; apply least privilege, need-to-know, and segregation of duties. Best practice: role-based access controls with regular permission audits.

nodes.label: Regulatory Compliance and Auditability
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data protection building block. Regulations such as GDPR, HIPAA, and the EU Data Act require transparent, traceable data processes and planned internal audits. SAP recommends SAP Information Lifecycle Management and SAP Data Privacy Integration. Best practice: keep an auditable record of all data processing activities.

nodes.label: Data Masking and Anonymization
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Data protection building block. Masking sensitive fields and anonymizing personal data complies with GDPR while preserving analytical performance; encryption secures intercepted data. SAP recommends SAP Data Custodian. Best practice: dynamic masking in non-productive systems and industry-standard encryption at rest and in transit.

nodes.label: SAP Analytics Cloud
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Analytics/visualization tool enabling business users to gain insights from data; part of SAP Business Data Cloud alongside SAP Datasphere and SAP Business Warehouse.

nodes.label: SAP Information Steward
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Recommended tool to profile data as part of data profiling and assessment.

nodes.label: SAP Data Custodian
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Recommended tool to apply security rules for data masking and anonymization.

nodes.label: SAP HANA Native Storage Extension
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: NSE adds a disk-based processing extension to SAP HANA's in-memory column store for warm data at low TCO; data remains part of the SAP HANA database with no application modification needed.

nodes.label: SAP Databricks
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Integrated into SAP Business Data Cloud through the strategic Databricks partnership; used to create AI models and genAI apps trained on business data.

nodes.label: SAP One Domain Model
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: A harmonized business data model SAP recommends using and extending for interoperability across SAP and non-SAP systems; also used in SAP Business Data Cloud to achieve a common interpretation of semantics across lines of business.

nodes.label: SAP Business AI
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: AI capabilities that are only as powerful as the data behind them; governed clean data feeds embedded SAP Business AI and the Joule copilot, which act in business context and amplify process outcomes. Built on the SAP Foundation Model and SAP Knowledge Graph.

nodes.label: SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.norm_label: sap data and analytics advisory methodology
nodes.rationale: Methodology to evaluate data strategies, develop solution architectures, and foster a data-driven culture.

nodes.label: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: The target platform for the clean core data white paper: customers migrating or transitioning from on-premises ERP to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

nodes.label: SAP Best Practices
nodes.author: SAP
nodes.rationale: Standardized data models and best-practice retention guidelines used to standardize data and define data lifecycles; the reference point for the Outdated Data KPI.

hyperedges.label: Clean Core Level Model Forms A/B/C/D
Wrapper Access Pattern for Non-Released Objects
Clean Core Governance and System Setup Pattern
ATC to RISE Dashboard Import Flow
Five Pillars of Clean Core Data
Governance and Maturity Practices for Clean Core Data
